Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to generate a DuckDB SQL query that performs the steps 1 For each case_id get the ordered sequence of activities sorted by timestamp 2 Group cases by their complete activity sequence that is the variant to identify unique process variants 3 Count cases per variant and determine top K variants by frequency 4 Return all events from original event_log where case_id is among the top K variant cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Step 1 determine ordered sequence of activities per case We can use string_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp to produce a path string Or we can use array_agg with ORDER BY and then cast to string Then we can group by that string to identify variants

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: activities may contain special characters using string_agg with delimiter might cause issues if the delimiter appears inside activity names Better use array_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp without casting just group by the array That directly identifies unique sequences

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: step 1 SELECT case_id array_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp AS variant FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 2 Group by variant the array and count cases WITH variants AS SELECT case_id array_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp AS variant_events FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id variant_counts AS SELECT variant_events COUNT AS frequency FROM variants GROUP BY variant_events

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Top K variants We need to rank by frequency descending select top K with ties handled If there are ties at the K th spot should we include all ties usually top K includes ties To include ties we could use a window function to rank and then include those with rank less than or equal to K

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt says top K variants by their frequency It does not specify tie breaking I will interpret it as including all variants that are within the top K ranks

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Plan Use WITH clauses variants as select case_id array_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp AS variant_array from event_log group by case_id variant_stats as select variant_array COUNT AS freq from variants group by variant_array ranked_variants as select variant_array freq DENSE_RANK OVER ORDER BY freq DESC AS rank from variant_stats top_k_variants as select variant_array from ranked_variants where rank less than or equal to K valid_cases as select v case_id from variants v join top_k_variants t on v variant_array equals t variant_array select e all from event_log e join valid_cases vc on e case_id equals vc case_id

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The original event_log will be filtered to only those cases belonging to the top K variants The final output should be the events all columns from those cases so SELECT e all

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We need to define K In DuckDB we can use a prepared statement with binding For the final answer I will define K as a variable in the query using a CTE for the parameter

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: WITH params AS SELECT 5 AS top_k variants AS SELECT case_id array_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp AS variant_array FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id variant_stats AS SELECT variant_array COUNT AS freq FROM variants GROUP BY variant_array ranked_variants AS SELECT variant_array freq RANK OVER ORDER BY freq DESC AS variant_rank FROM variant_stats top_k_variants AS SELECT variant_array FROM ranked_variants CROSS JOIN params WHERE variant_rank less than or equal to params top_k valid_cases AS SELECT v case_id FROM variants v JOIN top_k_variants t ON v variant_array equals t variant_array SELECT e all FROM event_log e JOIN valid_cases vc ON e case_id equals vc case_id

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The valid_cases CTE filters only those cases whose variant is in top_k_variants Then final select all columns from event_log for those case_id Thus the final query
